_doc: Food Inflammation Index (FII) parameter table — the single source of which per-100g signals enter the per-food inflammatory-potential score, each one's direction and weight. Adapted from the Food Inflammation Index idea (Wang 2024) to the composition columns this archive holds; directions are taken from open biomarker literature, NOT the licensed DII per-parameter effect scores. `dir` is +1 pro-inflammatory / -1 anti-inflammatory; `weight` scales a parameter's contribution. Read by src/core/fii.ts and scripts/build-inflammation-reference.mjs. The `fatQuality_g` parameter is DERIVED — (monoFat+polyFat) − (satFat+transFat) — so fat is scored once, by quality, instead of letting a food's saturated and unsaturated fractions cancel under independent z-scores.

parameters.cite: fat quality (MUFA+PUFA − SFA−trans), derived from the fat fractions: Wang 2024 FII

polyphenols (key anti driver): Cassidy 2015; Dryer-Beers 2024; Wang 2024 FII; Phenol-Explorer. fallback referent (0, 100 mg/100g) used while corpus coverage is sparse — a food at ~100 mg/100g reads polyphenol-rich (Pérez-Jiménez 2010)
